title: Instant Pot No-Soak Dry Beans
steps: Combine all ingredients in the Instant Pot. Do not fill the pot more than halfway full.
Secure the lid and set the pressure valve to the "Sealing" position.
Cook at high pressure using the times listed in Step 7. (Note: the pot will take 15–20 minutes to reach full pressure before the cooking timer begins.)

Release pressure — let it release naturally for as long as possible (20–30 minutes) before opening. You can do a quick release if needed, but natural release helps beans hold their shape and prevents foaming in the valve.
Check doneness. If beans are still firm, seal and cook at high pressure for another 5–10 minutes.
Use or store. Strain and use immediately, or cool and store in their cooking liquid for up to 5 days in the fridge or 3 months in the freezer.

cooking_times.red_kidney_beans: 25–30 minutes (boil for 10 minutes first to break down lectins)

key_tips: Don't skip the olive oil — it reduces foaming that can clog the pressure valve.
Salt early — it seasons the beans from the inside during cooking (start with 1 tsp, up to 2 tsp).
Use the lower end of the time range for firmer beans (salads, tacos); the higher end for softer beans (hummus, soup, refried beans).
For soaked beans, cooking time is roughly half the unsoaked time.
